reasoning: Based on the Wikipedia page "List of infectious diseases" as of May 29, 2025, there is no vaccine listed for Creutzfeldt–Jakob disease (CJD). CJD is a highly rare and uniquely challenging disease caused by prions, fundamentally differing from most viral/bacterial infections. No prion vaccine exists for humans, and research is mostly in early clinical therapy stages (like the PRN100 antibody treatment, which is a therapeutic rather than a prophylactic vaccine, and still faces extensive hurdles). Without any vaccine in late-stage clinical trials, and considering the base rate of typical vaccine development timelines (often >10 years), the probability of a CJD vaccine being developed and listed on Wikipedia by June–December 2025 is exceedingly close to zero. The extremely low probabilities account for minor edge cases such as a new therapeutic miscategorized as a vaccine on Wikipedia or a sudden emergency breakthrough, though both remain highly improbable. Uncertainty increases marginally for further-out dates just on base principle.
